How to fix segfault in GCC 13?

Posted by Jack Debug September 1, 2024

I'm getting a segmentation fault when compiling with GCC 13 on Ubuntu 22.04. The same code compiles fine with GCC 12. Has anyone else run into this?

Error occurs in template instantiation with variadic arguments.

Kate Compiler September 1, 2024

This is a known bug in GCC 13.1. It was fixed in 13.2. Try upgrading your compiler version.

Leo Sysadmin September 1, 2024

Can you share the minimal reproducer? Variadic template segfaults usually come from recursive instantiation depth limits.

Jack Debug September 2, 2024

Thanks Kate! Upgrading to 13.2 fixed it. Leo, I'll post the reproducer in case it helps others.

Mia Templates September 2, 2024

FYI, you can also add -ftemplate-depth=2048 as a workaround for the recursion depth issue.
